sanskrit:
समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम्।
विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति॥
transliteration:
samaṃ sarveṣu bhūteṣu tiṣṭhantaṃ parameśvaram |
vinaśyatsv avinaśyantaṃ yaḥ paśyati sa paśyati ||
literal_meaning: Equally in all beings, standing, the Supreme Lord; among the perishable, the imperishable—who sees, he sees.
contextual_meaning: True seeing is perceiving the deathless, equal awareness that inhabits every body while bodies themselves decay.
psychological_state: Post-dissociative integration: the mind has moved from identifying with transient forms to resting in the changeless witness, reducing death anxiety and implicit bias.
trigger_scenario: In an ICU you watch a loved one flat-line; simultaneously you sense the unchanged presence that was there before the heart stopped and is still there after.
anchor_text: See the same deathless awareness in every body.
